metadata.title: SPACEHOP Activity Communications Sector Attack Scenario

overview.brief_summary: SPACEHOP Activity targets the Communications sector with a series of attacks leveraging spearphishing, drive-by compromise, remote services exploitation, user execution, and automated exfiltration.

Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Internet-facing assets, Network Traffic, Command
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: Delivery Method, Network Traffic Flow (Mechanism), Command Execution (Mechanism)

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.description: The attacker sends targeted phishing emails to employees of telecommunications companies, compromising their email accounts.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.description: The victim visits a compromised website, leading to the execution of malware on their device.

stage.name: Execution
stage.description: The attacker leverages remote services like RDP or SSH to gain access to the victim's systems.

stage.name: Execution
stage.capec: CAPEC-663: Exploitation of Transient Instruction Execution
stage.description: The attacker executes malicious code on the victim's system using techniques like PowerShell or Python.

stage.name: Impact
stage.capec: CAPEC-24: Filter Failure through Buffer Overflow
stage.description: The attacker steals sensitive data from the victim's systems, including communications data.

Scenario.summary: SPACEHOP Activity poses a significant threat to the Communications sector, utilizing sophisticated tactics to breach initial access, execute malicious activities, and ultimately impact the victim's systems.
